title: Cryptographic Failures

explain.what: Sensitive data — especially passwords — is protected with a weak or broken algorithm. MD5 and SHA-1 are not password hashing algorithms. They are fast by design, which makes them easy to brute-force.
explain.example: Register with password 'password123'. The server stores its MD5 hash. An attacker who dumps your database can crack every hash in seconds using rainbow tables or GPU brute force.
explain.impact: Mass credential breach · account takeover across every service where users reuse passwords

vulnerable.annotation: ⚠ Passwords are hashed with MD5 — a fast hash designed for checksums, not passwords. Crackable in milliseconds.

fixed.annotation: ✅ bcrypt with cost factor 12 is designed for passwords — deliberately slow, salted, and resistant to GPU attacks.

demoHint: Register with any password. See the MD5 hash stored in the database, then paste it into crackstation.net to crack it instantly.
